articles.title: Over 150 Algorithms and Data Structures Demonstrated in JS
articles.description: Examples of many common algorithms (e.g. bit manipulation, Pascal’s triangle, Hamming distance) and data structures (e.g. linked lists, tries, graphs) with explanations. Available in eighteen other written languages too.
articles.author: Oleksii Trekhleb et al.

articles.title: ⚠️ The Shai-Hulud 2.0 npm Worm: Analysis, and What You Need to Know
articles.description: The next generation of a ‘worm’ we’ve previously encountered is back infecting more packages, exfiltrating developers' credentials, then republishing yet more packages to spread further. This is a good writeup of how it works.
articles.author: Tafani-Dereeper and Obregoso (Datadog)

articles.title: The Performance Inequality Gap in 2026
articles.description: Esteemed browser and Web standards expert Alex Russell looks at the state of client-side Web performance, what sort of bandwidth you should be taking into account, what devices people are using, and warns against ever-growing JavaScript bundle sizes. A lot of data here.
articles.author: Alex Russell

articles.title: Why Use React? (On the Frontend)
articles.description: Jeremy asks some big, potentially uncomfortable questions, but notes how React’s modern server-side powers are a real boon, while questioning React’s role on the frontend, where Preact might well suit you better.
articles.author: Jeremy Keith

articles.title: ▶  What are 'Invokers': Interactivity without JavaScript?
articles.description: The Invoker Commands API lets you assign behaviors to buttons. You can use JavaScript to create custom commands, however.
articles.author: Scott Tolinski

articles.title: How Vercel Built Its First Mobile App with React Native
articles.description: Vercel has built an iOS app for its v0 AI-powered app development tool using React Native and Expo. This is a detailed look at how they tackled certain issues to make the UX smooth and responsive.
articles.author: Fernando Rojo (Vercel)

articles.title: Wrangling My Email with Claude Code
articles.description: James shows how you can use Claude’s ‘agent skills’ to run a JavaScript app that fetches your email from Gmail for Claude Code to analyze.
articles.author: James Long

articles.title: FullCalendar: A Full Sized JavaScript Calendar Control
articles.description: Get a Google Calendar-style experience in your own apps. Has connectors for React, Vue and Angular, but can be used with plain JavaScript too. The base version is MIT licensed, but there’s a commercial version too with extra features.
articles.author: Adam Shaw

articles.title: Better Auth: A Comprehensive Authentication Framework for TypeScript
articles.description: A framework agnostic authentication and authorization framework that provides email and password-based auth, OAuth and social sign-in, account and session management, 2FA, and more. v1.4 was just released with stateless/database-free session management support.
articles.author: Better Auth

articles.title: Heat.js 4.5: A Heat Map Visualization Library
articles.description: Think the GitHub contributions heat map. No dependencies, small, responsive, and theme-able. There’s a live demo or its GitHub repo.
articles.author: William Troup

articles.title: Ant Design 6.0: The React UI Design Language and UI Library
articles.description: One of the bigger, more ‘corporate’ looking React component suites. v6 provides a smooth migration for v5 users and is focused on optimizations and React 19 compatibility.
articles.author: Ant Design Team

articles.title: TSDiagram: Diagrams as Code with TypeScript
articles.description: Draft diagrams quickly with TypeScript. Define your data models through top-level type aliases and interfaces and it automatically lays out the nodes in an efficient way. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Andrei Neculaesei

articles.description: Tanner Linsley tells the tale of two years running TanStack (well known for TanStack Start, Query, and Form, among others) as an open source organization.
The Piccalilli team has made the Introduction to Asynchronous JavaScript chapter of their JavaScript for Everyone course free to read online.
There's a variety of Black Friday deals offered by well-known members of the frontend community on courses and similar resources.
Node.js 24 is now a supported runtime on AWS Lambda (as nodejs24.x) and won't be deprecated until April 30, 2028.
Prettier 3.7 – The popular opinionated code formatter.
pnpm 10.24 – The fast, efficiency-focused package manager gets even faster with adaptive network concurrency.
Bun 1.3.3 – The popular JS runtime adds CompressionStream and DecompressionStream, upgrades to SQLite 3.51.0, and other minor enhancements.

Playwright 1.57 – Microsoft's browser/Web automation library now has a 'speedboard' tab in its HTML reports to show you your tests sorted by slowness. It also switches from Chromium to Chrome for Testing.
Valibot 1.2, Storybook 10.1, Next.js v16.0.5, Immer 11.0

articles.title: The Performance Inequality Gap in 2026
articles.description: Esteemed browser and Web standards expert Alex Russell looks at the state of client-side Web performance, what sort of bandwidth you should be taking into account, what devices people are using, and warns against ever-growing JavaScript bundle sizes. A lot of data here.
articles.author: Alex Russell

articles.title: Masonry: Things You Won’t Need a Library For Anymore
articles.description: Patrick Brosset, of the Edge team, takes a detailed look at this long-awaited feature, what it means for web developers, and how we can make use of it.
articles.author: Patrick Brosset

articles.title: Baseline in Action
articles.description: A new collection of articles and demos (announced here) showing you some of what’s possible with the features that are now Baseline. The latest looks at layered UI patterns.
articles.author: web.dev

articles.title: CSS-in-JS: The Great Betrayal of Frontend Sanity
articles.description: Alexander notes that what was supposed to “free us from global namespace nightmares and styling spaghetti” actually delivered performance issues and chaos. They make the case for ditching it in favor of native CSS solutions.
articles.author: Alexander T. Williams

articles.title: Non-Square Image Blur Extensions
articles.description: Ana walks us through creating an image blur effect using a handful of minimal CSS techniques, noting some constraints to keep in mind, and the browser support caveats.
articles.author: Ana Tudor

articles.title: ▶  Get the Core Right and the Resilient Code Will Follow
articles.description: In this roughly 40-minute talk Andy shares how improving core skills, planning, and the overall feedback loop, will ultimately result in writing better code.
articles.author: Andy Bell

articles.title: Anchor Positioning and the Inset-Modified Containing Block (IMCB)
articles.description: Bramus explains a fairly complex CSS positioning concept here, clarifying how the position-area and inset properties interact to define the positioning context for anchored elements. Interactive demos help explain things further.
articles.author: Bramus Van Damme

articles.title: Project Wallace: A Suite of CSS Analysis Tools
articles.description: This project has grown since we last included one of its offerings. It now has 13 different tools including an analyzer, code quality checker, design tokens extractor, and some one-off tools (minifier, specificity calculator, etc).
articles.author: Bart Veneman

articles.title: Myna: A Monospace Typeface Designed for Symbol-rich Programming
articles.description: The creator built this to combat the problem of often-used symbols being treated ‘like second-class glyphs’, with better alignment, weight, and clearer distinctions, to name a few features.
articles.author: Irfan Ali

articles.title: Framework7 9.0: Build iOS, Android and Desktop Apps with HTML
articles.description: An open-source framework for building full-featured apps with Web technologies, complete with an ecosystem of plugins and tools and the ability to hook into existing front-end frameworks. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Framework7

articles.title: Internet Archive Wayback Machine Link Fixer: Fix Link Rot on WordPress
articles.description: A useful WordPress plugin that will scan your posts (on save and any existing ones) and automatically create snapshots of outbound links (if not already available) to ensure links aren’t broken.
articles.author: Internet Archive

articles.title: capo.js 2.0: A Script to Optimize Element Order in Your Page's <head>
articles.description: Specifically this will show you the order of the elements in your <head> and suggest a different order for optimizing the perceived performance of the page.
articles.author: Rick Viscomi

articles.title: Design System Component Audit and Linting: A Figma Plugin for Design Systems
articles.description: Can do a quick scan or deep analysis to display data on your design system in Figma (e.g. unique components, missing metadata) along with component navigation and publishing status.
articles.author: Luis Ouriach

articles.title: A Bikini Bottom GSAP Snow Globe
articles.description: Here’s a festive CodePen demo recreating a scene from SpongeBob SquarePants, complete with Gary the Snail and the pineapple house. It makes use of the popular GSAP animation library. Here’s a brief write-up of how Leanne put it together.
articles.author: Leanne Renard codepen
